title: althaea australis
intro: Althaea australis, also known as the Tasmanian marshmallow, is a perennial plant native to Australia, belonging to the mallow family and characterized by its thick, fleshy roots and soft, velvety leaves. The plant's medicinal properties lie in its mucilaginous roots, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the digestive system and skin. The health benefits of Althaea australis include alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, soothing skin irritations and wounds, and serving as a natural demulcent to protect and calm mucous membranes. Althaea australis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
